title: dir2mcp_related
description: Nearest-neighbour segments for a given chunk or document ('more like this'), reusing the vector index.

input.description: Exactly ONE of chunk_id / rel_path identifies the source segment. Supplying neither or both is INVALID_FIELD.

chunk_id.description: The source segment: neighbours are ranked by similarity to this chunk's embedding vector. The source chunk itself is always excluded from hits.

rel_path.description: The source document (corpus-relative, normalized '/'): neighbours are ranked against the document's own chunk vectors. Chunks belonging to this document are always excluded from hits.

index.description: Which logical vector axis to search (SPEC §6.1). 'auto' matches the source segment's own index_kind.

exclude_same_document.description: For a chunk_id request: when true (default) all chunks of the source chunk's document are excluded ('other documents like this'); when false the source document's OTHER chunks may appear (the source chunk itself is always excluded). No-op for a rel_path request — a document's own chunks are always excluded (a document is never related to itself).

languages.description: Optional (SPEC §9.5): restrict hits to representations recorded in any of these BCP-47 languages (case-insensitive primary-subtag match). Absent/empty = no filtering.

date_from.description: Optional (SPEC §9.6): RFC 3339 timestamp or bare YYYY-MM-DD; exclude hits from documents dated before this (inclusive). Absent = open lower bound.

date_to.description: Optional (SPEC §9.6): RFC 3339 timestamp or bare YYYY-MM-DD; exclude hits from documents dated after this (inclusive). Absent = open upper bound.

source_chunk_id.description: Echo of the resolved source chunk_id when the request supplied chunk_id; omitted for a rel_path request.

source_rel_path.description: Echo of the resolved source document rel_path (present for a rel_path request, and also for a chunk_id request as the source chunk's document).

hits.description: Nearest-neighbour segments, ordered by descending similarity (ties broken by ascending chunk_id for determinism), with the source excluded (and same-document hits excluded per exclude_same_document). Same Hit shape as dir2mcp_search.
